Recipes Pairings Brew notes

quiet outside, caffeinated inside.

this page is just a small collection of my go-to, low effort, high reward recipes. From instant fixes to moka pot brews, cold brew, hazelnut hot coffee, and the very honest channi method.

Open the recipe cards Read my brew notes
accurate brain scan

Recipes I trust.

Each card opens up with ingredients, method, pairing, and one tiny trick that saves the drink from tasting like hostel pantry sadness.

What you need

  • 2 tsp instant coffee, Sleepy Owl hazelnut works nicely
  • 2 tsp sugar or honey
  • 2 tbsp hot water
  • Hot milk
  • Ice

How to make it

  1. Mix coffee, sugar and hot water until dissolved.
  2. Fill a glass with ice.
  3. Pour cold milk over it.
  4. Add the coffee mix and stir lightly.
Tiny trick: Use less water than usual. A concentrated coffee base keeps the drink from tasting watery after ice melts.
Pair it with: Best with butter toast, vanilla cake, or a very chaotic assignment deadline.

What you need

  • 1 moka pot shot or strong brewed coffee
  • Cold milk
  • Ice
  • 1 tsp sugar syrup, optional

How to make it

  1. Brew strong coffee in the moka pot.
  2. Let it cool for 3 to 5 minutes.
  3. Add ice to a glass.
  4. Pour milk first, then coffee for that pretty layered look.
Tiny trick: Do not pack moka pot coffee too tightly. Level it gently so the brew stays smooth, not bitter.
Pair it with: Best with banana bread, croissants, or portfolio edits at 2 AM.

What you need

  • 2 tbsp instant coffee
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp hot water
  • Cold milk
  • Ice

How to make it

  1. Whip coffee, sugar and hot water until thick and glossy.
  2. Add milk and ice to a glass.
  3. Spoon the whipped coffee on top.
  4. Mix before drinking unless you like suffering aesthetically.
Tiny trick: Use instant coffee only. Fresh coffee will not whip the same way.
Pair it with: Best with brownies, cookies, or a Pinterest board named future cafe.

What you need

  • Strong moka pot coffee or channi brewed coffee
  • 1 to 2 tbsp condensed milk
  • Ice

How to make it

  1. Add condensed milk to a glass.
  2. Pour hot strong coffee over it.
  3. Stir until smooth.
  4. Add ice and mix again.
Tiny trick: For channi method, steep coffee in hot water for 4 minutes, then strain through a fine sieve or cloth. It gives a rustic, strong brew without equipment.
Pair it with: Best with salted snacks, dark chocolate, or a long design critique.

What you need

  • 1 cup coarse ground coffee, Blue Tokai or Subko
  • 4 cups cold water
  • Milk or water to dilute
  • Ice

How to make it

  1. Mix coffee and cold water in a jar.
  2. Keep it in the fridge for 12 to 16 hours.
  3. Strain using a channi and cloth or a paper filter.
  4. Serve over ice with milk or water.
Tiny trick: Cold brew is forgiving, but grind size matters. Coarse grind makes it smoother and less muddy.
Pair it with: Best with granola, pancakes, or slow Sunday planning.

What you need

  • 2 tsp Sleepy Owl hazelnut instant coffee
  • 1 tsp brown sugar or honey, optional
  • 2 tbsp hot water
  • Hot milk
  • A pinch of salt

How to make it

  1. Dissolve hazelnut coffee and sugar in hot water.
  2. Warm the milk until steamy, not boiling.
  3. Pour milk into the coffee base.
  4. Add a pinch of salt and stir until smooth.
Tiny trick: Hazelnut tastes better with a pinch of salt. It makes the flavour feel more rounded and cafe-like.
Pair it with: Best with waffles, chocolate biscuits, or a quiet winter morning.

What you need

  • 1 strong coffee shot or 2 tsp instant coffee
  • 1 tbsp cocoa powder
  • 1 to 2 tsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp hot water
  • Cold milk
  • Ice

How to make it

  1. Mix cocoa, sugar and coffee with hot water until smooth.
  2. Add ice to a glass.
  3. Pour in milk.
  4. Add the mocha mix and stir well.
Tiny trick: Cocoa clumps easily. Make a smooth paste first, then add milk. This one detail changes everything.
Pair it with: Best with strawberries, brownies, or a good rom-com.

Best coffee accomplices.

Not everything needs a full cafe setup. Sometimes the correct answer is iced coffee, something sweet, and five minutes of peace.

Sweet

Brownies, vanilla cake, cookies, banana bread, waffles.

Salty

Cheese toast, salted chips, fries, peri peri makhana.

Fresh

Strawberries, oranges, yoghurt bowls, granola.

Work mode

A clean desk, headphones, cold coffee, and a task you have been avoiding.

My tiny coffee logic.

Instant coffee is for speed. Moka pot is for depth. Cold brew is for future planning. Channi method is the no equipment backup that deserves more respect.

Lazy iced
Moka latte
Cold brew
Mocha
Final verdict: coffee is not a beverage. It is a personality support system with ice cubes.

That is it. Go drink water too.

This page is intentionally unserious, but the recipes are serious enough to survive deadlines, design critiques, and early morning classes.
